claims.summary: Heavy telemetry decoding or classification might occur while holding shared BridgeState for writing.
claims.disposition: Verified decode and classification occur before the write lock and SQLite or trace persistence occurs after it.

claims.summary: Lock waiting, lock holding, and prewrite work need separate timing evidence.
claims.disposition: Verified current integration-health evidence records prewrite pipeline, write-lock wait, and write-lock hold independently.

claims.summary: Sequential handling or Pong and shutdown interaction could still produce observable delay under load.
claims.disposition: Route a bounded high-load and cooperative-shutdown observation using existing timing evidence before changing ordering.

claims.summary: Reconnect or availability-policy changes would alter live telemetry hearing.
claims.disposition: Preserve an exact Tier 5 operator-approval wait for reconnect, buffering, ordering, or telemetry-admission changes.
